name: Retro To-Do
description: A retro CRT terminal to-do app that looks like it's running on a 1980s green-phosphor monitor. Full CRT simulation — plastic bezel frame, barrel-distorted glass, scanlines, phosphor glow, screen flicker — wrapping a Kanban task manager with drag-and-drop, a calendar view, and delightful retro animations throughout. Built with React, Vite, Framer Motion, @dnd-kit, and Google Fonts (VT323, Share Tech Mono). All data in browser localStorage — no backend, no auth.

features.title: Set up React project with retro design system, fonts, and dependencies
features.description:
Initialize the Retro To-Do project in `projects/retro-to-do` using Vite + React + TypeScript.

**Dependencies to install:**
- `framer-motion` (animation library for React — entrance animations, layout transitions, drag ghosts, and orchestrated sequences)
- `@dnd-kit/core` and `@dnd-kit/sortable` (drag-and-drop for Kanban columns)
- `@dnd-kit/utilities` (transform helpers)
- `uuid` (for generating task IDs)

**Google Fonts to load (via `<link>` in index.html or @import in CSS):**
- `VT323` — the primary UI font. A pixel-perfect monospace font that looks like actual CRT phosphor text. Use for all body text, task titles, buttons, labels.
- `Share Tech Mono` — secondary font for the status bar and metadata. A cleaner monospace that still feels retro.
- `Press Start 2P` — accent font used sparingly for the app title in the header and the boot-up splash screen. Chunky 8-bit pixel font.

**CSS Custom Properties (define on `:root`):**
- `--crt-bg`: `#0a0a0a` (near-black screen background)
- `--crt-green`: `#33ff33` (bright phosphor green — primary text color)
- `--crt-green-dim`: `#1a8c1a` (dimmed green for secondary text, borders)
- `--crt-green-glow`: `rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.4)` (for text-shadow and box-shadow glow)
- `--crt-amber`: `#ffb000` (amber accent for warnings, high priority)
- `--crt-red`: `#ff3333` (red accent for errors, deletion)
- `--crt-cyan`: `#00ffff` (cyan accent for links, interactive highlights)
- `--bezel-color`: `#2a2520` (dark warm gray for the monitor bezel/plastic)
- `--bezel-highlight`: `#3d3630` (lighter bezel edge for 3D beveling)
- `--scanline-opacity`: `0.08` (opacity of scanline overlay)

**Global CSS reset:** Set `* { box-sizing: border-box; }`, `body { margin: 0; background: #1a1a1a; font-family: 'VT323', monospace; color: var(--crt-green); }`. Ensure all `input`, `textarea`, `select`, `button` elements inherit the VT323 font. Set a global `text-shadow: 0 0 8px var(--crt-green-glow)` on body text to create the phosphor glow effect that makes all text look like it's emitting light on a CRT.

**Out of scope:** No components yet — this is purely project scaffolding, dependency installation, and design token setup.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Vite dev server starts without errors (`npm run dev`)
- VT323, Share Tech Mono, and Press Start 2P fonts load from Google Fonts
- All CSS custom properties are defined on `:root`
- Body background is near-black (#0a0a0a or similar)
- Default text renders in VT323 with phosphor green color
- Text has a visible green glow via text-shadow
- framer-motion, @dnd-kit/core, @dnd-kit/sortable, and uuid are in package.json
- TypeScript compiles without errors
- Input/button elements inherit the VT323 monospace font

features.title: Implement task domain model and localStorage persistence layer
features.description:
Create the data layer for tasks, stored exclusively in `localStorage` (no server APIs, no auth).

**Task shape (TypeScript interface):**
- `id`: string — UUID v4 generated via the `uuid` package when a task is created.
- `title`: string — required, trim whitespace, max 200 characters.
- `description`: string — optional, max 2000 characters.
- `priority`: `'low' | 'medium' | 'high'` — stored as lowercase string literals.
- `dueDate`: `string | null` — optional ISO 8601 date string (`YYYY-MM-DD`) for the user's local calendar date. `null` means no due date.
- `status`: `'todo' | 'in_progress' | 'completed'` — maps to Kanban columns: To-Do, In Progress, Completed.
- `createdAt`: string — ISO 8601 timestamp set once on creation, never modified.
- `updatedAt`: string — ISO 8601 timestamp updated on every mutation (edit, move, priority change).
- `order`: number — integer for deterministic ordering within a column. When a task is created it gets `Math.max(...columnTasks.map(t => t.order), -1) + 1`. When moved to a new column, it appends to the bottom.

**Storage key:** `retro-todo:tasks:v1`. If the stored JSON is corrupt or missing, fall back to an empty array silently.

**Exported API (React hook `useTasks`):**
- `tasks`: the full array of tasks.
- `addTask(payload)`: creates a new task with `status: 'todo'`, generates ID, sets timestamps.
- `updateTask(id, partial)`: merges partial fields, updates `updatedAt`.
- `deleteTask(id)`: removes from array.
- `moveTask(id, newStatus)`: changes status, appends to bottom of new column, updates `updatedAt`.
- `reorderTask(id, newOrder)`: changes order within the same column.
- `getTasksByStatus(status)`: returns filtered + sorted by `order`.
- `clearCompleted()`: removes all tasks with `status === 'completed'`.

**Persistence:** Every mutation writes the full task array to localStorage synchronously. On hook mount, read from localStorage.

**Out of scope:** No sync, no IndexedDB, no encryption — localStorage only.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Task TypeScript interface/type is exported with all specified fields
- `useTasks` hook returns tasks array and all mutation functions
- New tasks default to `status: 'todo'` with generated UUID and timestamps
- `priority` validates to only 'low' | 'medium' | 'high'
- `dueDate` accepts null/undefined for no due date and YYYY-MM-DD strings
- Tasks persist across full page reloads (same browser, same origin)
- Corrupt localStorage JSON recovers gracefully to empty array without errors
- `updatedAt` changes on edits and moves; `createdAt` is immutable after creation
- `deleteTask` removes the task from storage immediately
- `moveTask` changes status and appends task to the end of the destination column
- `clearCompleted` removes all completed tasks and persists the change
- `getTasksByStatus` returns tasks sorted by their `order` field ascending

features.title: Build CRT monitor frame with plastic bezel, power LED, and inner screen
features.description:
Create the outer "monitor" container that wraps the entire application content, making it look like the app is displayed inside a physical 1980s CRT monitor.

**Structure (three nested layers):**

1. **Outer bezel** — A large `div` centered on the page (or filling the viewport on mobile). Background: `var(--bezel-color)` (#2a2520). Border-radius: 20px on outer corners. Apply a 3D beveled look using layered `box-shadow`: a lighter edge on top-left (`var(--bezel-highlight)`) and darker edge on bottom-right to simulate plastic depth. The bezel should be about 24-32px thick on each side on desktop. At the bottom-center of the bezel, add a subtle embossed brand label — a small `<span>` with text like "RETROTASK 3000" in uppercase Share Tech Mono, 10px, slightly lighter than the bezel color, with a faint `text-shadow` to simulate stamped/embossed plastic lettering. Next to the brand label (bottom-right area), place a tiny circular "power LED" — a 10px round `div` with `background: #33ff33`, `border-radius: 50%`, and a pulsing `box-shadow: 0 0 6px 2px var(--crt-green-glow)` animation (opacity oscillates between 0.5 and 1.0 over 2s, infinite, ease-in-out).

2. **Glass screen area** — Inside the bezel. Background: `var(--crt-bg)`. Border-radius: 12px (simulating the slightly curved CRT glass edge). Apply `overflow: hidden` so all content and effects are clipped to the screen bounds. This is the area where scanlines, glow, and app content render.

3. **Content area** — A scrollable inner `div` inside the glass screen where the actual app components (header, board, calendar) will mount. Use `position: relative; z-index: 1` so it sits beneath the scanline overlay.

**Responsive behavior:** On viewports below 640px width, reduce the bezel to ~12px thick and hide the brand label. The monitor should never exceed `100vw` or `100vh` — on mobile the bezel becomes a thin dark border. On desktop (>1024px), the monitor can have a max-width of ~1200px, centered horizontally with some breathing room around it showing the page background (dark gray #1a1a1a).

**Accessibility:** The bezel and decorations are `aria-hidden="true"`. The content area has `role="main"`.

**Out of scope:** Scanlines, glow effects, and boot animation — those are separate features layered on top of this frame.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- A visible bezel/frame surrounds the app content, clearly evoking a physical CRT monitor
- Bezel has a 3D beveled appearance via box-shadow (lighter top-left, darker bottom-right)
- Brand label text (e.g. 'RETROTASK 3000') is visible at the bottom of the bezel in Share Tech Mono
- A small green LED indicator pulses with a glow animation near the bottom of the bezel
- Inner screen area has near-black background and rounded corners
- Content renders inside the screen area without clipping issues
- On viewports below 640px, bezel shrinks to ~12px and brand label hides
- On desktop, monitor is centered with a max-width around 1200px
- Bezel decorations have aria-hidden='true'
- Content area has role='main'
- No horizontal overflow at any viewport width from 320px to 1920px

features.title: Apply CRT screen effects: scanlines, phosphor glow, barrel distortion, and vignette
features.description:
Layer four purely visual effects onto the CRT glass screen area created in the monitor frame feature. These effects sit on top of app content via absolutely-positioned overlays with `pointer-events: none` so they don't block interaction.

**Effect 1 — Scanlines:**
A full-screen overlay `div` positioned absolutely over the glass screen. Use a CSS `repeating-linear-gradient` to create horizontal lines: `repeating-linear-gradient(0deg, transparent, transparent 1px, rgba(0, 0, 0, var(--scanline-opacity)) 1px, rgba(0, 0, 0, var(--scanline-opacity)) 2px)`. This creates 1px transparent lines alternating with 1px semi-transparent black lines, simulating CRT scan lines. Set `z-index: 10` and `pointer-events: none`. The overlay should cover the full inner screen area.

**Effect 2 — Phosphor glow / bloom:**
Add a subtle pulsing ambient glow to the entire screen. Apply to the glass screen container: `box-shadow: inset 0 0 60px 10px rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.05), inset 0 0 120px 40px rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.02)`. This creates a soft green halo around the edges of the screen interior, like phosphor bleed on a real CRT. Animate the glow intensity with a slow keyframe (8s cycle, ease-in-out) that varies the opacity between 0.03 and 0.07 — a barely perceptible breathing effect.

**Effect 3 — Barrel distortion (screen curvature):**
Simulate the convex curvature of a CRT screen. If the browser supports it, apply a very subtle CSS `perspective` + `rotateX(1deg)` on the glass screen, combined with a radial-gradient vignette that darkens the corners. Alternatively, use a `border-radius` of ~50% / 5% on a pseudo-element overlay with a very faint radial gradient to suggest curvature. Keep it subtle — the goal is to suggest curvature, not to warp content to the point of illegibility. About 2-4% visual curve.

**Effect 4 — Vignette:**
A radial-gradient overlay from `transparent` at center to `rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4)` at edges, creating darkened corners like a real CRT. Use `background: radial-gradient(ellipse at center, transparent 60%, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.4) 100%)`. Same `pointer-events: none` and high `z-index`.

**Performance:** All effects must use CSS only (no JavaScript per-frame rendering). Use `will-change: opacity` on animated elements. Respect `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` — disable the glow pulse and any flicker animation when reduced motion is preferred, but keep the static scanlines and vignette.

**Out of scope:** Boot animation, content styling — this feature is only the overlay effects.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Horizontal scanlines are visible across the entire screen area as thin semi-transparent stripes
- A subtle green glow/bloom emanates from the screen edges, slowly pulsing in intensity
- Screen corners are darkened by a vignette effect, creating natural CRT edge falloff
- Some form of barrel distortion or curvature suggestion is applied to the screen
- All overlay effects have pointer-events: none so clicking through to content works
- Text and interactive elements remain fully readable and clickable beneath all overlays
- `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` disables or dampens animated effects (glow pulse) but keeps static overlays
- No JavaScript runs per-frame for these effects — all CSS-based
- Effects do not cause visible layout shift or content reflow
- Body text at 16px+ remains legible through the scanline overlay

features.title: Create boot-up sequence animation on first app load
features.description:
When the user first loads the app (or refreshes), show a 3-4 second boot-up animation sequence that mimics a 1980s computer powering on, before revealing the actual app content. Use Framer Motion for orchestrating the multi-step sequence.

**Sequence (timed steps):**

**Step 1 (0.0s–0.8s) — Screen power-on:**
The CRT screen starts completely black. A thin bright green horizontal line appears in the center of the screen (like a CRT warming up) — 1px height, 60% width, centered, with a strong glow. This line rapidly expands vertically to fill the screen (scale Y from 0.01 to 1 over ~500ms with an ease-out curve), simulating the phosphor tubes warming up.

**Step 2 (0.8s–2.2s) — POST/boot text:**
Green monospace text (VT323, 14px) appears line by line, each line delayed ~150ms, simulating a BIOS Power-On Self-Test:
```
RETROTASK 3000 BIOS v2.1
Copyright (c) 1987 RetroSoft Inc.

Memory Test: 640K OK
Detecting storage... localStorage found
Loading task database...

All systems nominal.
Starting RETROTASK...
```
Each line types out character by character at ~30ms per character to create a typewriter effect. Use a blinking green block cursor (`█`) at the end of the current line.

**Step 3 (2.2s–3.0s) — Splash screen:**
Clear the POST text. Display a large ASCII art logo centered on screen, rendered in `Press Start 2P` font at ~20px:
```
╔══════════════════════╗
║   R E T R O T A S K  ║
║       3 0 0 0        ║
╚══════════════════════╝
```
The logo fades in with a green glow burst (box-shadow animation). Hold for ~800ms.

**Step 4 (3.0s–3.5s) — Transition to app:**
The splash fades out and the actual app content fades in. Use Framer Motion's `AnimatePresence` to crossfade.

**State management:** Store a `hasBooted` flag in React state (not localStorage — we want the boot animation on every page load for the retro experience, but provide a way to skip: clicking anywhere during the sequence immediately jumps to the app). The boot sequence component unmounts after completion.

**Accessibility:** If `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`, skip the entire boot sequence and show the app immediately.

**Out of scope:** The actual app content — this feature only handles the boot animation overlay.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- On fresh page load, a boot sequence animation plays for ~3-4 seconds before showing the app
- The sequence includes a CRT power-on effect (horizontal line expanding to fill screen)
- POST/BIOS text appears line by line with a typewriter effect and blinking block cursor
- A splash screen with 'RETROTASK 3000' displays briefly after the POST text
- The boot sequence transitions smoothly to the actual app content
- Clicking anywhere during the boot sequence skips directly to the app
- `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` skips the entire boot sequence immediately
- Boot animation uses Framer Motion (not raw CSS keyframes) for orchestration
- No layout shift or flash of unstyled content when transitioning from boot to app
- Boot sequence does not block React hydration or cause performance issues

features.title: Build retro app header with terminal title, view toggle, and status bar
features.description:
Create the app header and status bar that sit inside the CRT screen area.

**Header bar (top of screen):**

A horizontal bar with `background: rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.08)` and a 1px solid bottom border in `var(--crt-green-dim)`. Padding: 12px 16px. Flex layout with three sections:

- **Left: App title** — Text "RETROTASK 3000" in `Press Start 2P` font, ~14px, `var(--crt-green)`. After the title text, render a blinking block cursor (`█`) that toggles visibility every 500ms via CSS animation. The cursor and title together should look like a command prompt.

- **Center: View toggle** — Two retro-styled tab buttons: "[BOARD]" and "[CALENDAR]". Square brackets are literal characters in the button text, rendered in VT323 at 16px. The active tab has `background: var(--crt-green)` with `color: var(--crt-bg)` (inverted — green background, black text). The inactive tab has `background: transparent`, `color: var(--crt-green-dim)`, and a 1px dashed border in `var(--crt-green-dim)`. On hover, inactive tabs get a subtle glow: `box-shadow: 0 0 8px var(--crt-green-glow)`. Include a smooth transition on background/color (150ms).

- **Right: Add Task button** — Text "+ NEW TASK" in VT323, 16px. Background: transparent. Border: 2px solid `var(--crt-green)`. Padding: 8px 16px. On hover: background fills to `var(--crt-green)`, text inverts to `var(--crt-bg)`, and the button gets a glow (`box-shadow: 0 0 12px var(--crt-green-glow)`). Transition: 150ms ease. On click, the button briefly flashes (opacity 0.5 for 100ms then back to 1.0) as tactile feedback.

**Status bar (bottom of screen):**

A thin bar (28px height) at the absolute bottom of the screen area. Background: `rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.05)`. Border-top: 1px solid `var(--crt-green-dim)`. Font: Share Tech Mono, 12px, `var(--crt-green-dim)`.

Content (flex, space-between):
- Left: Task counts — "TODO: X | IN PROGRESS: Y | DONE: Z" where X/Y/Z are live counts from the task store.
- Right: Current date/time in retro format — "1987-03-15 14:30:00" style (use actual current date). Update every second.

**Accessibility:** Header is `<header>` landmark. Buttons have visible focus outlines (`outline: 2px solid var(--crt-cyan)` on `:focus-visible`). Tab toggle uses `role="tablist"` with `role="tab"` on each button and `aria-selected`.

**Out of scope:** The actual board/calendar content area — this feature builds only the header and status bar chrome.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Header bar renders at top of the CRT screen with green-tinted background and bottom border
- App title 'RETROTASK 3000' displays in Press Start 2P font with a blinking block cursor
- View toggle shows [BOARD] and [CALENDAR] tabs with clear active/inactive styling
- Active tab has inverted colors (green bg, black text); inactive has dashed border
- '+ NEW TASK' button is visible with green border, and inverts colors on hover with glow
- Status bar at bottom shows live task counts (TODO: X | IN PROGRESS: Y | DONE: Z)
- Status bar shows current date/time updating every second in retro format
- All interactive elements have visible focus outlines on keyboard navigation
- View toggle uses proper ARIA tablist/tab roles with aria-selected
- Header uses semantic <header> element
- Layout works without overflow on viewports from 375px wide and up

features.title: Build Kanban board with three retro-styled columns and empty states
features.description:
Render the Kanban board view inside the main content area (between header and status bar). Three columns wired to the task store's `getTasksByStatus`.

**Column layout:**
Three columns in a horizontal flex row with equal width (flex: 1). Gap: 16px between columns. Padding: 16px around the board. On viewports below 640px, stack columns vertically. Each column has `min-height: 300px` so empty columns have presence.

**Column styling:**
Each column has:
- A top border made with ASCII-style box-drawing characters: a `<div>` showing `╔══ TO-DO ══════════════╗` (etc.) in VT323 at 14px, `var(--crt-green)`. The column label is embedded in the top border line. Use CSS to ensure the `═` characters fill available width (or truncate gracefully).
- Background: `rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.02)` — very faintly tinted, enough to separate from the screen background.
- Left and right borders: 1px solid `var(--crt-green-dim)` styled as `║` characters or simple CSS borders.
- Bottom border: matching the top with `╚═══════════════════════╝`.
- Inside: a scrollable area (`overflow-y: auto`, custom scrollbar styled thin and green) that holds the task cards.

**Column headers:**
- "TO-DO" column: label in `var(--crt-green)`
- "IN PROGRESS" column: label in `var(--crt-cyan)` (#00ffff)
- "COMPLETED" column: label in `var(--crt-green-dim)` (dimmer, completed tasks feel resolved)

**Empty state:**
When a column has zero tasks, show centered placeholder text in `var(--crt-green-dim)` at 14px VT323:
- TO-DO: `> No tasks queued_` (with a blinking underscore cursor)
- IN PROGRESS: `> Idle...` 
- COMPLETED: `> Nothing completed yet`

**Task count badge:**
Next to each column header label, show the count in square brackets: e.g. `╔══ TO-DO [3] ══════╗`. The count updates reactively.

**Out of scope:** Task cards (separate feature), drag-and-drop (separate feature), calendar view.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Three columns render side by side labeled TO-DO, IN PROGRESS, and COMPLETED
- Column headers use ASCII box-drawing style borders (╔═╗ / ║ / ╚═╝ or equivalent)
- Each column header shows a task count in square brackets that updates reactively
- Empty columns show distinct placeholder text with retro styling
- TO-DO empty state shows a blinking cursor/underscore
- Tasks from the store appear in the correct column based on their status
- Columns are scrollable when content overflows vertically
- On viewports below 640px, columns stack vertically
- Column backgrounds are subtly tinted, distinguishable from the main screen background
- Board layout has consistent 16px padding and gaps
- Custom scrollbar on columns is styled thin and green (on browsers that support it)

features.title: Create retro task card with priority badges, metadata, and hover glow
features.description:
Build the individual task card component that renders inside Kanban columns. Each card represents one task from the store.

**Card container:**
- Background: `rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.04)` (very subtle green tint on black).
- Border: 1px solid `var(--crt-green-dim)`.
- Padding: 12px.
- Margin-bottom: 8px between cards.
- Border-radius: 2px (sharp, retro).
- On hover: border brightens to `var(--crt-green)`, and apply `box-shadow: 0 0 12px rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.15), inset 0 0 12px rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.05)` — a phosphor glow that makes the card look like it's being highlighted on the CRT. Transition: 200ms ease.
- Cursor: pointer on hover.

**Card content layout (vertical stack):**

1. **Top row** (flex, space-between):
   - **Priority badge** — A small inline label:
     - LOW: text `[LOW]` in `var(--crt-green-dim)`, no special styling.
     - MEDIUM: text `[MED]` in `var(--crt-amber)` (#ffb000) with `text-shadow: 0 0 6px rgba(255, 176, 0, 0.4)`.
     - HIGH: text `[!!!]` in `var(--crt-red)` (#ff3333) with `text-shadow: 0 0 8px rgba(255, 51, 51, 0.5)` and a subtle CSS blink animation (opacity oscillates 1.0 → 0.6 → 1.0 every 1.5s). The blinking red `[!!!]` badge creates urgency.
   - **Due date** (if present) — Text like `DUE: 2024-03-15` in Share Tech Mono, 12px, `var(--crt-green-dim)`. If overdue (date is in the past and task is not completed), render in `var(--crt-red)` with prefix `OVERDUE:` instead.

2. **Title** — The task title in VT323, 16px, `var(--crt-green)`. Truncate with ellipsis if longer than 2 lines (`display: -webkit-box; -webkit-line-clamp: 2; overflow: hidden`).

3. **Description** (if present) — First 100 characters of description in VT323, 13px, `var(--crt-green-dim)`. Truncated with `...` if longer. Only show if description exists.

4. **Bottom row** (flex, space-between):
   - **Created timestamp** — `Share Tech Mono`, 11px, `var(--crt-green-dim)`, showing relative time: "2h ago", "3d ago", etc.
   - **Action buttons** (visible on card hover only, using CSS `:hover` on parent):
     - Edit button: `[EDIT]` text in `var(--crt-cyan)` — clicking opens the edit flow.
     - Delete button: `[DEL]` text in `var(--crt-red)` — clicking triggers deletion confirmation.

**Completed task styling:** If the task's status is `completed`, apply `opacity: 0.5` and add a CSS `text-decoration: line-through` on the title. The phosphor glow on hover is dimmer.

**Framer Motion entrance:** When a card first renders (mount), animate from `opacity: 0, y: 10` to `opacity: 1, y: 0` over 200ms. Use `layout` prop on the card container so cards animate smoothly when reordering.

**Out of scope:** Edit modal, delete confirmation, drag-and-drop — handled in separate features.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Task cards render inside their correct Kanban column based on task status
- Each card shows the task title, priority badge, and created-at relative timestamp
- Priority badges use distinct styling: [LOW] dim green, [MED] amber with glow, [!!!] red with blink
- Due dates display when present; overdue dates show in red with 'OVERDUE:' prefix
- Description shows first 100 chars when present, truncated with ellipsis
- Cards glow on hover with a phosphor-green box-shadow effect
- [EDIT] and [DEL] action buttons appear only when hovering the card
- Completed tasks have reduced opacity and line-through on the title
- Cards animate in on mount (fade + slide from below) using Framer Motion
- Cards use Framer Motion layout animation for smooth reordering
- Card text is readable at all viewport widths from 375px+

features.title: Build task creation modal with CRT-themed form controls and validation
features.description:
Wire the '+ NEW TASK' header button to open a task creation modal dialog.

**Modal backdrop:**
A full-screen overlay with `background: rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.85)` — nearly opaque, simulating a CRT screen dimming. Apply the scanline effect to the backdrop too (same repeating-linear-gradient as the main screen). The backdrop fades in over 200ms via Framer Motion.

**Modal container:**
Centered on screen. Max-width: 480px. Background: `var(--crt-bg)` (#0a0a0a). Border: 2px solid `var(--crt-green)`. Box-shadow: `0 0 30px rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.2), 0 0 60px rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.1)` — strong phosphor glow around the modal. Padding: 24px. The modal scales in from 0.95 to 1.0 with opacity 0→1 over 250ms (Framer Motion `initial/animate`).

**Modal header:**
Text `> NEW TASK ENTRY_` in VT323, 20px, `var(--crt-green)` with a blinking block cursor at the end. Below the header, a thin `═══════════════════` divider line in `var(--crt-green-dim)`.

**Form fields (all VT323 font, 16px):**

1. **Title** (required):
   - Label: `TITLE:` in `var(--crt-green-dim)`, 14px.
   - Input: `<input type="text">` with `background: transparent`, `border: none`, `border-bottom: 1px solid var(--crt-green-dim)`, `color: var(--crt-green)`, `caret-color: var(--crt-green)`, `text-shadow: 0 0 6px var(--crt-green-glow)`. On focus, border-bottom brightens to `var(--crt-green)` with a glow. Placeholder text: `Enter task title...` in `var(--crt-green-dim)` at 50% opacity.
   - Max length: 200 characters. Show remaining count: `[187/200]` in 12px Share Tech Mono at the right edge.

2. **Description** (optional):
   - Label: `DESCRIPTION:` same style.
   - `<textarea>` with same transparent/underline styling as title input. 3 rows minimum height, auto-grows. Placeholder: `Optional details...`

3. **Priority** (required, default: medium):
   - Label: `PRIORITY:`
   - Three inline toggle buttons: `[LOW]` `[MED]` `[HIGH]`. These are styled like the header view toggle: active selection has inverted colors (green bg, black text), inactive have dashed borders. MED is selected by default. Clicking a button selects it and deselects others. The HIGH button's active state uses `var(--crt-red)` background instead of green.

4. **Due Date** (optional):
   - Label: `DUE DATE:`
   - `<input type="date">` styled to match the retro aesthetic — override the browser's date picker as much as possible with green text on black background. If the browser date picker can't be fully styled, that's acceptable.

**Action buttons (bottom of modal, flex row, justify-end):**
- `[CANCEL]` — `var(--crt-green-dim)` text, transparent background, dashed border. On hover: solid border. Closes modal without saving.
- `[CREATE]` — Same style as the header's + NEW TASK button (green border, inverts on hover with glow). Validates and saves.

**Validation:** On clicking CREATE: if title is empty or whitespace-only, show error text `> ERROR: Title is required_` in `var(--crt-red)` below the title field, with a brief screen-flash effect (the modal background flashes `rgba(255,51,51,0.1)` for 150ms). Don't submit.

**On successful creation:** Call `addTask` from the hook with the form data. Close the modal with a fade-out. The new task should appear in the TO-DO column immediately.

**Keyboard:** ESC closes the modal. Enter in the title field submits (if valid). Focus trap: Tab cycles between form fields and buttons only.

**Framer Motion:** Use `AnimatePresence` for mount/unmount animations on the modal.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Clicking '+ NEW TASK' in the header opens the modal with a scale+fade animation
- Modal has a glowing green border and phosphor glow box-shadow
- Modal backdrop is nearly opaque with scanline overlay effect
- Title input is required; submitting empty shows red error text with screen flash
- Title shows a character counter [X/200] that updates as user types
- Description textarea is optional with auto-grow behavior
- Priority toggle defaults to [MED] and allows selecting LOW/MED/HIGH
- HIGH priority active state uses red instead of green
- Due date input accepts optional date selection
- [CANCEL] closes the modal without creating a task
- [CREATE] with valid data adds a task to the TODO column and closes modal
- ESC key closes the modal
- Focus is trapped inside the modal while it's open
- All form inputs use VT323 font with green text on transparent/black background
- Form inputs have glowing green focus states

features.title: Add inline task editing with retro text input fields
features.description:
Allow users to edit an existing task by clicking the `[EDIT]` button on a task card (or double-clicking the card). The card transforms in-place into an editable form without opening a separate modal.

**Behavior on entering edit mode:**
When edit is triggered, the card content smoothly transitions (using Framer Motion `layout` animation, ~200ms) to show editable fields in place of the static display. The card border brightens to `var(--crt-cyan)` (#00ffff) to indicate edit mode, with `box-shadow: 0 0 15px rgba(0, 255, 255, 0.2)` — a cyan glow instead of the usual green.

**Editable fields (same retro input styling as the creation modal):**
1. **Title** — Pre-filled `<input>` with the current title. Same underline style, auto-focused on entering edit mode.
2. **Description** — Pre-filled `<textarea>`, auto-growing.
3. **Priority** — Same `[LOW]` `[MED]` `[HIGH]` toggle buttons, pre-selected to current value.
4. **Due Date** — Pre-filled date input, or empty if no due date.

**Action buttons (replace the normal card footer):**
- `[SAVE]` — Validates (title required), calls `updateTask(id, changes)`, exits edit mode. Button styled in `var(--crt-cyan)` to match the edit mode accent.
- `[CANCEL]` — Discards changes, exits edit mode, card returns to normal display.

**Keyboard shortcuts in edit mode:**
- `Ctrl+Enter` or `Cmd+Enter`: Save
- `Escape`: Cancel edit

**Transition back to display mode:** The card smoothly transitions back (Framer Motion) — border returns to `var(--crt-green-dim)`, glow fades, editable fields morph back to static text.

**Constraint:** Only one card can be in edit mode at a time. Entering edit on card B while card A is being edited should auto-cancel card A's edit (no save).

**Out of scope:** Drag-and-drop while in edit mode — disable drag on the card currently being edited.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Clicking [EDIT] on a task card transforms it into inline editable fields
- Double-clicking a card also enters edit mode
- Card border changes to cyan with a cyan glow to indicate edit mode
- Title, description, priority, and due date are all editable with pre-filled values
- [SAVE] validates title (required) and persists changes via updateTask
- [CANCEL] discards unsaved changes and returns to display mode
- Ctrl+Enter / Cmd+Enter saves; Escape cancels
- Only one card can be in edit mode at a time
- Entering edit on another card auto-cancels the currently editing card
- Transitions between display and edit mode are smooth (Framer Motion)
- The title input auto-focuses when entering edit mode
- updatedAt timestamp updates on successful save

features.title: Implement drag-and-drop task movement with phosphor trail ghost effect
features.description:
Enable users to drag task cards between Kanban columns to change their status. Use `@dnd-kit/core` and `@dnd-kit/sortable` for the interaction.

**Setup:**
- Wrap the Kanban board in a `<DndContext>` provider.
- Each column is a `<SortableContext>` droppable zone.
- Each task card is a draggable item via `useSortable`.

**Drag start:**
When the user grabs a card (mouse down + move, or touch start + move):
- The original card stays in place but dims to `opacity: 0.3` with a dashed border (placeholder showing where the card was).
- A **drag overlay** appears under the cursor, rendered via `@dnd-kit`'s `<DragOverlay>`. This overlay is a copy of the card but with enhanced retro styling:
  - Border: 2px solid `var(--crt-green)` (brighter than normal).
  - Box-shadow: `0 0 20px var(--crt-green-glow), 0 0 40px rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.15)` — a strong phosphor glow trail.
  - Slight scale: `transform: scale(1.03)` — lifted off the surface.
  - Add a faint, brief green afterimage trail effect: apply a CSS `filter: drop-shadow(0 4px 8px rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.3))` that creates the illusion of the phosphor persistence (the ghosting that old CRTs had when objects moved).

**Drag over column:**
When the drag overlay enters a different column's drop zone, that column's border brightens to `var(--crt-green)` and its background lightens slightly to `rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.06)` — a visual cue that this column will accept the drop. The other columns dim slightly.

**Drop (valid):**
On drop into a new column: call `moveTask(id, newStatus)` to update the task's status and persist. The card animates into its new position via Framer Motion's `layout` animation (smooth ~200ms slide). The column highlight fades back to normal.

**Drop (invalid/cancel):**
If the user drops outside any column or releases without moving, the overlay snaps back to the original position and the placeholder un-dims. No state changes.

**Keyboard-accessible fallback:**
Add visible `[←]` and `[→]` arrow buttons on each card (shown on hover/focus alongside [EDIT] and [DEL]). Clicking `[→]` moves the task to the next column (todo → in_progress → completed); `[←]` moves to the previous column. These buttons are always available as an alternative to drag-and-drop. Cards being edited (in edit mode) should not be draggable.

**Out of scope:** Reordering within the same column (cards just append to bottom of new column). Touch-specific optimizations beyond what @dnd-kit provides by default.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Task cards can be picked up and dragged between Kanban columns using mouse/touch
- Dragged card shows a glowing phosphor-green overlay with scale and drop-shadow
- Original card position shows a dimmed placeholder during drag
- Target column highlights (brightened border + lightened background) when drag enters
- Dropping on a valid column updates the task status in localStorage and survives reload
- Invalid drops (outside columns) cancel cleanly with no state changes
- updatedAt timestamp updates on successful move
- [←] and [→] arrow buttons on cards provide keyboard-accessible column movement
- Cards in edit mode cannot be dragged
- Framer Motion layout animation smoothly repositions cards after a drop
- @dnd-kit DndContext, SortableContext, and DragOverlay are properly set up

features.title: Add task deletion with retro blinking warning confirmation dialog
features.description:
When the user clicks `[DEL]` on a task card, show a retro-styled confirmation dialog before permanently deleting the task.

**Confirmation dialog:**
This is a small modal/popover that appears near the card being deleted (or centered on screen on mobile). It should feel like a retro system warning.

**Visual design:**
- Background: `var(--crt-bg)` (#0a0a0a).
- Border: 2px solid `var(--crt-red)` (#ff3333).
- Box-shadow: `0 0 20px rgba(255, 51, 51, 0.3)` — red phosphor glow.
- Padding: 16px.
- Max-width: 320px.

**Content:**
- Header: `⚠ WARNING ⚠` in VT323, 18px, `var(--crt-red)`, with a CSS blink animation (opacity alternates between 1.0 and 0.3 every 600ms). The warning text blinks to draw attention.
- Message: `Delete task: "[task title truncated to 40 chars]"?` in VT323, 14px, `var(--crt-green)`.
- Subtext: `This action cannot be undone.` in VT323, 12px, `var(--crt-red)` at 70% opacity.
- Two buttons:
  - `[Y] DELETE` — `var(--crt-red)` border, red text. On hover: red background, black text, red glow. On click: deletes the task.
  - `[N] CANCEL` — `var(--crt-green-dim)` border, dim text. On hover: brightens. On click: closes dialog without deleting.

**Behavior:**
- Pressing `Y` key confirms deletion; `N` key or `Escape` cancels.
- On confirmed deletion: the task card should animate out — shrink vertically to 0 height and fade to 0 opacity over 300ms (Framer Motion exit animation) — then the task is removed from the store.
- The confirmation dialog itself enters with a quick shake animation (CSS or Framer Motion — 3px horizontal oscillation for 300ms) to feel like an urgent system alert.

**Clear Completed shortcut:**
Also add a `[CLEAR DONE]` button in the Completed column header (next to the column label). This triggers a similar confirmation: `Clear all X completed tasks?` with the same warning style. On confirm, calls `clearCompleted()`. Each completed card animates out in sequence (staggered 50ms delay).

**Out of scope:** Undo functionality — deletion is permanent.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Clicking [DEL] on a task card opens a retro warning confirmation dialog
- Dialog has red border with red phosphor glow and blinking '⚠ WARNING ⚠' header
- Dialog shows the task title being deleted (truncated if long)
- [Y] DELETE button confirms and removes the task from the store
- [N] CANCEL button closes the dialog without deleting
- Y key and N key work as keyboard shortcuts for confirm/cancel
- Escape key cancels the deletion
- Deleted task card animates out (shrink + fade) before being removed from DOM
- Confirmation dialog enters with a quick shake animation
- [CLEAR DONE] button in Completed column header deletes all completed tasks
- Clear completed shows its own confirmation with count of tasks to be deleted
- Completed tasks animate out in staggered sequence when cleared
- Deletions persist to localStorage immediately

features.title: Add task completion celebration with retro pixel fireworks animation
features.description:
When a task is moved to the 'Completed' column (either by drag-and-drop or by clicking the [→] button from In Progress), trigger a brief celebratory animation.

**Fireworks effect:**
Use Framer Motion to animate 8-12 small particle elements that burst outward from the position where the task card landed in the Completed column.

**Particle design:**
Each particle is a small square (4-6px) in a random retro color: `var(--crt-green)`, `var(--crt-cyan)`, `var(--crt-amber)`, or white. Particles are styled as pixel-art squares (no border-radius — sharp corners, like 8-bit confetti).

**Animation sequence (Framer Motion):**
1. Particles spawn at the card's center position (use the card's DOM rect).
2. Each particle animates outward in a random direction (random angle 0-360°, random distance 40-120px) while also moving downward slightly (simulating gravity).
3. Particles fade from `opacity: 1` to `opacity: 0` over the animation duration.
4. Each particle has a slightly randomized duration (400-700ms) and a slight rotation.
5. Total effect lasts ~700ms. Particles unmount after animation completes.

**Additional flair:**
- On the task card itself, briefly flash the border to `var(--crt-cyan)` and show a small `✓ DONE` badge overlay for 500ms before the card settles into its normal completed styling (dimmed + strikethrough).
- Play the completion effect only once per move — if the user drags a task out of Completed and back in, the effect plays again.

**Performance:** Use `position: fixed` for particles so they don't affect layout. Clean up particle DOM nodes after animation. Use `will-change: transform, opacity` for smooth 60fps animation.

**Accessibility:** Skip the fireworks entirely when `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce` is active — just show the ✓ DONE badge briefly instead.

**Out of scope:** Sound effects — visual animation only.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Moving a task to the Completed column triggers a burst of pixel-square particles
- Particles are small squares in retro colors (green, cyan, amber, white)
- Particles animate outward from the card position and fade out over ~500-700ms
- The completed card briefly shows a '✓ DONE' badge before settling into dimmed state
- The card border briefly flashes cyan on completion
- Effect plays each time a task enters the Completed column
- Particles use position: fixed and don't affect layout or cause scrollbars
- Particle DOM nodes are cleaned up after animation completes
- prefers-reduced-motion: reduce disables the fireworks (just shows badge)
- Animation runs at smooth 60fps without jank
- Effect works for both drag-and-drop and arrow-button task movement

features.title: Build month calendar view with retro ASCII grid and due-date task placement
features.description:
Create an alternate view (toggled via the [CALENDAR] tab in the header) that shows tasks on a monthly calendar grid.

**Calendar layout:**
A full month grid rendered inside the main content area. The grid has 7 columns (one per weekday) and 5-6 rows depending on the month.

**Header row (weekday labels):**
A row of 7 cells labeled `SUN`, `MON`, `TUE`, `WED`, `THU`, `FRI`, `SAT` in VT323, 14px, `var(--crt-cyan)`. Cells separated by `│` box-drawing characters or 1px solid borders in `var(--crt-green-dim)`.

**Month navigation:**
Above the grid, a navigation bar:
- Left: `[◄ PREV]` button
- Center: Month and year label, e.g. `═══ MARCH 2024 ═══` in VT323, 18px, `var(--crt-green)`, with `═` decorative borders.
- Right: `[NEXT ►]` button
- Navigation buttons use the same retro hover style (glow + invert).

**Day cells:**
- Each cell shows the day number in the top-left corner, VT323, 12px, `var(--crt-green-dim)`.
- Background: `var(--crt-bg)` with 1px borders in `var(--crt-green-dim)` (creating a grid).
- Today's date: highlighted with a brighter border (`var(--crt-green)`) and faintly glowing background (`rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.06)`).
- Days outside the current month (padding days): dimmed to near-invisible (`opacity: 0.2`).
- Cell height: at least 80px on desktop to show task previews.

**Task placement:**
Tasks with a `dueDate` appear in the corresponding day cell. Show up to 3 tasks per cell:
- Each task is a single line: priority badge (`[!]` for high, `[·]` for medium, nothing for low) + truncated title (first 15 chars + `…`). Font: VT323, 11px.
- Color by status: `var(--crt-green)` for todo, `var(--crt-cyan)` for in-progress, `var(--crt-green-dim)` with strikethrough for completed.
- If more than 3 tasks on a day, show `+N more` link in `var(--crt-amber)`. Clicking it could expand the cell or show a tooltip with all tasks (implementer chooses).

**Tasks without due dates** do not appear on the calendar. Show a note below the grid: `> N task(s) have no due date and are not shown` in `var(--crt-green-dim)`, 12px.

**Empty calendar (no tasks with due dates):**
Show the grid normally but with a centered overlay message: `> No dated tasks found. Add due dates to see tasks here._` with blinking cursor.

**Clicking a task** on the calendar should trigger the same inline edit behavior as the board view (if that feature is implemented).

**Responsive:** On mobile (<640px), reduce cell height to 60px and show only task count dots (colored circles instead of text).

**Out of scope:** Drag-and-drop to change due dates on the calendar, week/day views.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Calendar grid renders with 7 weekday columns and correct number of rows for the month
- Weekday header row shows SUN through SAT labels
- Navigation bar shows current month/year with working PREV and NEXT buttons
- Navigation works correctly across month and year boundaries
- Today's date cell is highlighted with brighter border and subtle glow
- Tasks with due dates appear in the correct day cells
- Task entries show priority indicator and truncated title
- Tasks are color-coded by status (green=todo, cyan=in-progress, dim=completed)
- Days with more than 3 tasks show a '+N more' indicator
- Tasks without due dates are excluded with a note below the grid
- Days outside the current month are visually dimmed
- Clicking a task on the calendar allows editing
- On mobile, cells shrink and show dots instead of text
- Empty state shows appropriate message when no tasks have due dates

features.title: Add view toggle with CRT static channel-change transition animation
features.description:
Wire the [BOARD] and [CALENDAR] toggle buttons in the header to switch between the Kanban board view and the calendar view. Add a retro CRT channel-change transition effect.

**State management:**
Store the active view (`'board' | 'calendar'`) in React state. Also persist the preference to localStorage under key `retro-todo:ui:viewPreference` so it survives page reloads. If the stored value is invalid, default to `'board'`.

**Channel-change transition (the star of this feature):**
When the user clicks a view toggle button, don't just swap content — simulate the visual noise you'd see when changing channels on an old TV:

1. **Phase 1 (0-150ms) — Static burst:** The current view content rapidly dissolves. Overlay a full-screen noise/static pattern on the content area. Implement this as a `<canvas>` element (or a CSS background image of generated noise) that covers the content area. The noise is black-and-white random pixels, flickering (re-draw every 50ms for 3 frames). Add a brief audio-like visual: white horizontal lines that jump vertically (optional — if too complex, just the noise is fine).

2. **Phase 2 (150-250ms) — Black screen:** Content area goes fully black for ~100ms. A single thin horizontal line (like a CRT losing sync) may flash across the middle.

3. **Phase 3 (250-400ms) — New view fades in:** The new view content fades in from black, with a slight vertical sync roll effect (content slides down by ~20px and settles into position). The noise clears. The transition is complete.

**Total transition time: ~400ms.** Fast enough to feel snappy, slow enough to be seen.

**Implementation:** Use Framer Motion's `AnimatePresence` with `mode="wait"` to orchestrate the exit of the old view and entrance of the new view. The static noise overlay can be a separate component that mounts/unmounts during the transition.

**Accessibility:** If `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`, skip the noise effect and just do a simple 150ms crossfade.

**The + NEW TASK button** in the header must remain functional regardless of which view is active. Clicking it always opens the task creation modal.

**Out of scope:** The actual content of each view (Board and Calendar) — those are separate features. This feature is the toggle logic and transition animation.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Clicking [BOARD] or [CALENDAR] in the header switches the main content view
- Active tab styling updates immediately on click (inverted colors on active tab)
- A CRT channel-change transition plays during the view switch (~400ms)
- Transition includes a static/noise burst phase, brief blackout, and fade-in of new view
- View preference persists to localStorage and is restored on page reload
- Invalid stored preference defaults to board view
- + NEW TASK button opens the creation modal from either view
- prefers-reduced-motion: reduce replaces the noise effect with a simple crossfade
- Transition does not cause layout shift or content jump after completion
- Switching views rapidly (spam-clicking) does not break state or cause errors

features.title: Implement command-line style search and filter bar with blinking cursor
features.description:
Add a search/filter interface that looks like a retro command-line prompt, positioned between the header and the board/calendar content.

**Search bar layout:**
A single-line input area styled as a terminal prompt. Full width of the content area, height ~36px. Background: `rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.03)`. Border-bottom: 1px solid `var(--crt-green-dim)`.

**Prompt design:**
- Left side: fixed text `SEARCH>` in VT323, 14px, `var(--crt-green-dim)` — this is not editable, it's a label.
- Right side: the actual text input, styled as: `background: transparent`, `border: none`, `color: var(--crt-green)`, `caret-color: var(--crt-green)`, `font-family: VT323`, `font-size: 16px`. Full width of remaining space.
- The native caret is styled green. After the input text (or at the end of the empty input), a blinking green block cursor character (`█`) is shown via CSS pseudo-element or an adjacent span (toggles visibility every 500ms). Hide this custom cursor when the input is focused (the native caret takes over).

**Search behavior:**
- Searches filter tasks in real-time as the user types (debounced 150ms).
- Search matches against task `title` and `description` (case-insensitive substring match).
- On the Board view: non-matching cards are hidden (with Framer Motion exit animation — fade out over 150ms). Column task counts update to reflect filtered results.
- On the Calendar view: non-matching tasks are hidden from day cells.
- When search is cleared (empty input), all tasks reappear (with Framer Motion entrance animation).

**Filter chips (below the search bar when active):**
When the user has an active search, show a row of quick-filter chip buttons:
- `[ALL]` `[TODO]` `[IN PROGRESS]` `[DONE]` — filter by status. Each is a small retro button (VT323, 12px). Active filter has inverted colors (like the view toggle). Multiple status filters can be active (toggleable). Default: all active.
- `[LOW]` `[MED]` `[HIGH]` — filter by priority. Same toggle behavior.
- Chips appear with a slide-down animation when search text is entered, and slide up when cleared.

**Keyboard shortcut:** Pressing `/` (forward slash) anywhere on the page focuses the search input (unless already typing in another input/textarea). Pressing `Escape` while in the search input clears it and blurs.

**Empty results:** If filtering produces zero matching tasks, show: `> No tasks match query: "[search term]"_` in the content area.

**Out of scope:** Advanced query syntax (like `priority:high`), saved searches.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- A search bar styled as a terminal prompt (SEARCH> prefix) appears between header and content
- Typing in the search bar filters tasks in real-time by title and description
- Filtering is case-insensitive substring matching with 150ms debounce
- Non-matching task cards animate out; matching cards remain visible
- Column task counts update to reflect filtered results
- Status filter chips (ALL/TODO/IN PROGRESS/DONE) appear when search is active
- Priority filter chips (LOW/MED/HIGH) appear when search is active
- Filter chips are toggleable and multiple can be active simultaneously
- Pressing '/' focuses the search input from anywhere on the page
- Pressing Escape in the search input clears it and removes focus
- Empty search results show an appropriate 'no matches' message
- The blinking block cursor is visible when the search input is empty/unfocused
- Search works on both Board and Calendar views
- Clearing the search restores all tasks with entrance animation

features.title: Add retro toast notifications with typewriter character-by-character text
features.description:
Create a notification/toast system for action confirmations that uses a retro typewriter text effect.

**Toast container:**
Toasts appear at the bottom-center of the CRT screen area (above the status bar), stacked vertically with newest on top. Max 3 visible toasts at once — oldest auto-dismisses if a 4th arrives.

**Toast design:**
- Background: `var(--crt-bg)` (#0a0a0a).
- Border: 1px solid `var(--crt-green)` (or `var(--crt-amber)` for warnings, `var(--crt-red)` for errors).
- Padding: 10px 16px.
- Min-width: 280px, max-width: 400px.
- Box-shadow: `0 0 10px` in the border's color (matching glow).
- Font: VT323, 14px.

**Typewriter text effect:**
When a toast appears, its message text types out character by character at ~25ms per character. Use a React effect that reveals characters one at a time by slicing the message string. During typing, show a blinking `█` cursor at the end. Once fully typed, the cursor blinks 2 more times then disappears.

**Toast types and their messages:**
- **Success** (green border): `> Task created: "[title]"` / `> Task updated` / `> Task moved to [column]` / `> Completed tasks cleared`
- **Warning** (amber border): `> Task deleted: "[title]"`
- **Error** (red border): `> ERROR: [message]`

**Prefix symbols:**
- Success: `✓` in `var(--crt-green)`
- Warning: `⚠` in `var(--crt-amber)`  
- Error: `✗` in `var(--crt-red)`

**Auto-dismiss:** Each toast auto-dismisses after 3 seconds (counting from when the typewriter effect completes). A thin progress bar at the bottom of the toast (2px height, green/amber/red matching the toast type) shrinks from 100% to 0% width over the 3-second countdown, giving visual feedback of time remaining.

**Dismiss interaction:** Clicking a toast dismisses it immediately.

**Framer Motion animations:**
- Enter: slide up from below + fade in (y: 20 → 0, opacity: 0 → 1, duration: 200ms).
- Exit: slide down + fade out (y: 0 → 20, opacity: 1 → 0, duration: 150ms).
- Use `AnimatePresence` for smooth add/remove of toasts.

**Hook API:** Export a `useToast()` hook that returns `showToast(message, type)` where type is 'success' | 'warning' | 'error'. Wire this into the task operations: show success toast on create, update, move; warning toast on delete; error toast on validation failure.

**Accessibility:** Toasts should be in an `aria-live="polite"` region so screen readers announce them. For error toasts, use `aria-live="assertive"`.

**Out of scope:** Toast action buttons (like Undo), toast history.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Toasts appear at the bottom-center of the screen, above the status bar
- Toast text types out character by character with a blinking cursor (~25ms per char)
- Success toasts have green border/glow, warnings amber, errors red
- Each toast type has a distinct prefix symbol (✓, ⚠, ✗)
- Toasts auto-dismiss after 3 seconds with a visible countdown progress bar
- Maximum 3 toasts visible at once; oldest dismissed when 4th arrives
- Clicking a toast dismisses it immediately
- Toasts slide up on enter and slide down on exit (Framer Motion)
- useToast hook is wired into task operations (create, update, move, delete)
- Toast container uses aria-live for screen reader announcements
- Error toasts use aria-live='assertive'
- Typewriter cursor blinks twice after text completes, then disappears

features.title: Create idle CRT screensaver with Matrix-style green character rain
features.description:
After a period of user inactivity, activate a screensaver that displays falling green characters (Matrix digital rain effect) over the app content, evoking both the CRT aesthetic and the iconic 1999 movie.

**Idle detection:**
Track the last user interaction (mouse move, key press, touch, scroll) via global event listeners. After 90 seconds of no interaction, activate the screensaver. Store the idle timeout in a ref, reset it on any interaction.

**Screensaver overlay:**
A full-screen `<canvas>` element positioned absolutely over the CRT screen content, `z-index: 20` (above scanlines). Background: `rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.92)` — nearly opaque, dimming the app behind it.

**Matrix rain implementation:**
Use a `<canvas>` and `requestAnimationFrame` for the falling character effect:
- Divide the canvas width into columns (each ~14px wide, matching the VT323 character width).
- Each column has a "drop" — a y-position that advances downward at a random speed (1-3px per frame).
- At each drop position, render a random character from a set of: katakana characters (ア-ン), digits (0-9), and ASCII symbols (@#$%&*). Each character is drawn in VT323 font.
- Character colors: the leading character (bottom of the trail) is bright white `#ffffff`. The next 3-5 characters above it are bright green `#33ff33`. Characters further up the trail fade to progressively dimmer green. Characters at the top of the trail fade to near-invisible `#0a0a0a`.
- When a drop reaches the bottom of the canvas, reset it to the top with a new random speed and a random delay (0-100 frames) before starting again.
- Characters in each column change randomly every few frames (random interval 5-15 frames), creating the flickering mutation effect.

**Deactivation:**
Any user interaction (mouse move, key press, touch, click) immediately deactivates the screensaver:
- The canvas fades out over 300ms (opacity 1 → 0).
- The canvas unmounts.
- The idle timer resets.

**Optional subtle text:** After 10 seconds of screensaver, fade in small text at the bottom-center: `Move mouse or press any key...` in VT323, 12px, `var(--crt-green-dim)` at 40% opacity.

**Performance:** The canvas animation should target 30fps (not 60) to reduce CPU usage during idle. Use `requestAnimationFrame` with frame-skipping. Stop the animation loop when the screensaver is deactivated.

**Accessibility:** If `prefers-reduced-motion: reduce`, use a static Matrix-style pattern instead of animated rain (draw one frame and stop), or skip the screensaver entirely.

**Out of scope:** Multiple screensaver themes (only Matrix rain for now), user-configurable idle timeout.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- After 90 seconds of no user interaction, a Matrix-style rain screensaver activates
- Falling characters include katakana, digits, and ASCII symbols
- Leading characters are bright white, trailing characters fade from green to dim
- Characters randomly mutate/change as they fall
- Any user interaction (mouse, keyboard, touch) immediately deactivates the screensaver
- Screensaver fades out smoothly over ~300ms on deactivation
- Canvas animation runs at ~30fps for performance
- Animation loop stops completely when screensaver is deactivated
- A subtle 'press any key' hint appears after 10 seconds of screensaver
- prefers-reduced-motion: reduce shows a static pattern or skips the screensaver
- Screensaver renders on a canvas element above app content with near-opaque background
- Screensaver does not interfere with app state — all tasks remain intact

features.title: Implement keyboard shortcuts with retro terminal help overlay
features.description:
Add a comprehensive set of keyboard shortcuts for power users, with a retro-styled help overlay that lists all available commands.

**Keyboard shortcuts (global, unless an input/textarea is focused):**

| Key | Action |
|-----|--------|
| `N` | Open the New Task modal |
| `/` | Focus the search bar |
| `?` | Toggle the keyboard shortcuts help overlay |
| `1` | Switch to Board view |
| `2` | Switch to Calendar view |
| `Escape` | Close any open modal/overlay, clear search, or deactivate screensaver |

**Help overlay design:**
Pressing `?` opens a centered overlay that lists all shortcuts. Styled as a retro terminal help screen:

- Background: `var(--crt-bg)` with `rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.04)` tint.
- Border: 2px solid `var(--crt-green)` with phosphor glow box-shadow.
- Max-width: 500px. Padding: 24px.
- Title: `╔═══ KEYBOARD COMMANDS ═══╗` in VT323, 18px, `var(--crt-cyan)`.
- Below the title, a horizontal rule: `════════════════════════════` in `var(--crt-green-dim)`.

**Shortcut list layout:**
Two-column layout inside the overlay:
- Left column: the key, shown in a small retro "keycap" style — `display: inline-block`, `border: 1px solid var(--crt-green)`, `padding: 2px 6px`, `min-width: 24px`, `text-align: center`, `background: rgba(51, 255, 51, 0.08)`. Font: Share Tech Mono, 14px.
- Right column: the action description in VT323, 14px, `var(--crt-green)`.
- Each row has 8px vertical spacing.

**Footer:** `Press [?] or [ESC] to close` in VT323, 12px, `var(--crt-green-dim)`.

**Animation:** Overlay enters with Framer Motion: fade in + scale from 0.95 to 1.0, duration 200ms. Exits with fade out + scale to 0.95.

**Backdrop:** Same nearly-opaque black backdrop as the task creation modal.

**Implementation:** Use a global `keydown` event listener (React `useEffect` on the app root). Check `document.activeElement` — if it's an `<input>`, `<textarea>`, or `[contenteditable]`, don't trigger shortcuts (except Escape). Prevent default on matched keys to avoid browser conflicts (e.g., `/` shouldn't start browser search).

**Out of scope:** Customizable shortcuts, vim-style navigation between tasks.
features.acceptanceCriteria:
- Pressing '?' opens a keyboard shortcuts help overlay
- Help overlay lists all available shortcuts with retro keycap styling
- Help overlay has ASCII box-drawing border and CRT-style glow
- Pressing 'N' opens the New Task modal
- Pressing '/' focuses the search bar
- Pressing '1' switches to Board view, '2' to Calendar view
- Escape closes any open modal, overlay, or clears search
- Shortcuts do not fire when user is typing in an input or textarea
- '/' shortcut prevents browser's default find-in-page behavior
- Help overlay enters/exits with Framer Motion scale+fade animation
- A backdrop appears behind the help overlay
- Footer text shows how to close the overlay
